articles.title: Huge crowds in Mashhad as Iran's late supreme leader is buried
articles.summary: Ali Khamenei was buried at the Imam Reza shrine, Iran's holiest Shia Muslim site, ending six days of public mourning ceremonies.

articles.title: Ukrainian agent accused of murdering Monaco bomb suspect changes story
articles.summary: Two men with links to Ukraine's security services are accused of killing Anastasiia Berezovska and dumping her body in woods.

articles.title: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
articles.summary: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

articles.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
articles.summary: Headed for Taiwan and south-eastern China, the 1,000 km-wide Bavi is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades.

articles.title: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says
articles.summary: It follows similar landings of reusable rockets by US-owned companies SpaceX and Blue Origin.

articles.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn in mega US share sale
articles.summary: The shares are set to start trading on the Nasdaq on Friday in what will be the largest ever debut by a foreign firm.

articles.title: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
articles.summary: The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

articles.title: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
articles.summary: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

articles.title: Greek F-16 fighter jet crash-lands at busy Zakynthos airport
articles.summary: The runway is closed after the jet reportedly landed without deploying its landing gear before bursting into flames.

articles.title: Ex-Olympian pleads not guilty to Reflecting Pool vandalism charges
articles.summary: A former US Olympian canoeist denies accusations that he deliberately damaged the lining of the refurbished Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
